notes: OpenCode's --session chaining IS the session-resume mechanism: each `opencode run --session <id>` after the first joins the existing SQLite session row. Spec asserts: (1) session born in ready, (2) first turn cycles ready→working→ready, (3) the SAME ses_… id appears again after the first process exits, (4) second turn cycles ready→working→ready on that same id, (5) final ready. The 'same session_id reappears' assertion is what the daemon must observe — and it does, because the SQLite row persists across PID lifetimes.

text: First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Same ses_… id re-appears in ready when second `opencode run --session` joins the existing SQLite session
